solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def reorderedPowerOf2(self, n: int) -> bool:
        digits = sorted(str(n))
        power_of_two = 1
        while power_of_two < n:
            power_of_two <<= 1
            if sorted(str(power_of_two)) == digits:
                return True
        return False
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution efficiently checks if a given number can be reordered to form a power of two. It iterates through powers of two up to the input number, sorting the digits of each power of two and comparing them to the sorted digits of the input. This approach leverages the binary nature of powers of two and the fact that their digit representations have a unique pattern.
